name: Peña Beneita Route
name_original: Ruta de Peña Beneita — circular desde Nuez de Aliste
description: Hike the moderate 7.3-mile Route of Peña Beneita from Nuez de Aliste, exploring ancient petroglyphs, a reservoir, and scenic valleys.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Aliste traverse a region in the northwest of Zamora, Spain, characterized by its significant portion of the Sierra de la Culebra mountain range. This area features varied terrain, from deep valleys to gentle peaks, with trails winding through pine and heather forests. The landscape is heavily wooded, and several rivers, including the Castro and Tera, have their sources here. Best easy hiking trails around Aliste The most popular easy hiking route is Abelón's Waterfalls and San Vicente Hermitage Viewpoint - circular from Abelón, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This route features waterfalls and scenic viewpoints. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11) — circular route in the Montesinho Natural Park, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This trail explores the natural park with gentle elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Hiking around Aliste is defined by wooded slopes, diverse river valleys, and the gentle peaks of the Sierra de la Culebra. The network offers options for various ability levels, focusing on easy hiking trails. The routes in Aliste are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Aliste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails can I find in Aliste?
A: Aliste offers a good selection of easy hiking trails, with over 15 routes specifically categorized as easy. These trails are perfect for those looking for a relaxed outdoor experience amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: What makes Aliste a good destination for easy hikes?
A: Aliste, particularly within the Sierra de la Culebra, provides diverse and accessible landscapes. You'll find trails winding through pine and heather forests, gentle peaks, and deep valleys. The region's natural features, including wooded slopes and several rivers, create a scenic and engaging environment for easy walks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Aliste?
A: The easy hiking trails in Aliste are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil natural settings, the varied landscapes, and the opportunity to immerse themselves in the region's unique environment.

Q: Are there any easy trails in Aliste that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can enjoy easy hikes that lead to beautiful waterfalls. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026842087/abelon-s-waterfalls-and-san-vicente-hermitage-viewpoint-circular-from-abelon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abelón's Waterfalls and San Vicente Hermitage Viewpoint - circular from Abelón&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic waterfalls. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the impressive La Palia Waterfall.

Q: Can I find easy circular hiking routes in Aliste?
A: Absolutely. Many easy trails in Aliste are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026842087/abelon-s-waterfalls-and-san-vicente-hermitage-viewpoint-circular-from-abelon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abelón's Waterfalls and San Vicente Hermitage Viewpoint - circular from Abelón&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424/the-portuguese-side-of-rio-de-onor-pr-11-circular-route-in-the-montesinho-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11) — circular route in the Montesinho Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123258047/rio-esla-from-puente-quintos-circular-by-camino-mozarabe-sanabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on easy hikes in Aliste?
A: Aliste, particularly within the Sierra de la Culebra, is renowned for its rich biodiversity. While hiking, you might spot deer and wild boars. The region is also famous for having one of the highest densities of Iberian wolves on the Iberian Peninsula, making it a significant spot for wildlife observation. Various bird species also inhabit the wooded and rocky areas.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks to see along the easy trails?
A: Yes, several easy trails offer access to interesting landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025267178/cistercian-monastery-of-santa-maria-from-granja-de-moruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria from Granja de Moruela&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/769731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintos Bridge Over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña el Pingón Cave and Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hiking in Aliste?
A: Aliste offers beauty throughout the year. Autumn is particularly magical, with an explosion of colors in the forests and the availability of local delicacies like chestnuts and mushrooms. The rutting season for deer in late September and early October is also an impressive spectacle. Spring brings lush greenery, while winter can offer snow-capped summits.

Q: Are there easy trails suitable for families with children?
A: Many of Aliste's easy trails are suitable for families, featuring gentle terrain and manageable distances. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt; are shorter and offer engaging natural features like waterfalls, making them ideal for younger hikers.

Q: Can I find easy trails with good viewpoints in Aliste?
A: Yes, Aliste's varied terrain includes several spots with excellent views. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026842087/abelon-s-waterfalls-and-san-vicente-hermitage-viewpoint-circular-from-abelon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abelón's Waterfalls and San Vicente Hermitage Viewpoint - circular from Abelón&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a viewpoint. Additionally, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5840033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Hullón&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic vistas of the landscape.

Q: Are there any easy trails that follow river paths?
A: Aliste is home to several rivers, and you can find easy trails that trace their banks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123258047/rio-esla-from-puente-quintos-circular-by-camino-mozarabe-sanabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a pleasant walk through riverine landscapes. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283120270/rio-duero-desde-pino-del-oro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Duero desde Pino del Oro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Duero river area.

Q: What is the typical duration for an easy hike in Aliste?
A: The easy hikes in Aliste vary in length, but many can be completed within 1.5 to 2 hours. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026842087/abelon-s-waterfalls-and-san-vicente-hermitage-viewpoint-circular-from-abelon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abelón's Waterfalls and San Vicente Hermitage Viewpoint - circular from Abelón&lt;/a&gt; takes approximately 1 hour 35 minutes, while &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123258047/rio-esla-from-puente-quintos-circular-by-camino-mozarabe-sanabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 1 hour 50 minutes.
tips: After crossing a narrow valley we arrive at Boca Barreros where we can admire the reservoir that makes up the dam of the same name. To your left is the Barreros Fountain signposted.
Beautiful picture that the Arroyo del Valle gives us as we walk the path that accompanies it for a long and picturesque stretch.

Arriving at Fuente Ferrada, we can rest in the picnic area sheltered in the shade of hundred-year-old holm oaks, accompanied by the sound of the waters and enjoying the peace that is breathed in this place.
Nice bridge that crosses the Arroyo del Valle. Nearby we will find a recreational area in case we want to take a break and observe the small waterfalls that form in the place.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Route of Peña Beneita?
faqs.answer: The Route of Peña Beneita is a circular trail that typically takes around 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete. This estimate is for the full 11.7 km route.

faqs.question: Where does the Route of Peña Beneita trail start?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in the charming village of Nuez de Aliste. You can begin your hike from the village center, perhaps near the recently restored church in the Plaza de la Moral.

faqs.question: Is the Route of Peña Beneita suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is considered a low-difficulty route, making it suitable for hikers of varying experience levels. The paths are generally good and well-marked.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally characterized by good paths, making it a straightforward hike. You'll traverse a narrow valley, walk alongside the Neu River, and pass through a 'cortafuegos' (firebreak) section. The route is well-marked with white and yellow stripes.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Route of Peña Beneita?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and points of interest. You'll pass by the Boca Barreros Reservoir, follow the Arroyo del Valle, and cross the traditional Puente de la Plaza. Notable historical features include ancient petroglyphs and an abandoned mine at Peña Beneita. There's also a pleasant Picnic Area at Fuente Ferrada.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Route of Peña Beneita?
faqs.answer: This trail is suitable for year-round enjoyment due to its low difficulty. However, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler, potentially muddier conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Route of Peña Beneita trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it is generally considered a dog-friendly trail. For the safety and comfort of other hikers and local wildlife, please keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Route of Peña Beneita. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or resting areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are designated resting areas. The Pontón features a traditional stone bridge and the ruins of an old flour mill, while the Picnic Area at Fuente Ferrada offers a shaded spot under ancient holm oaks.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Calle Colaguita trail.

faqs.question: Is there parking available in Nuez de Aliste?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can find parking within the village of Nuez de Aliste. As it's a small village, street parking is generally available, allowing easy access to the trailhead.
